This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
John Condry papers
Condry, John C.
Cornell University. Center for Research on the Effects of Television
23-13-3741
.4 cubic feet. .4 cubic feet.
1967-1993. Publications Includes publications and papers written by Condry concerning his research on the psychology of television and its impact on children. Collection material in English 6571201
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Eileen Keating
Date completed:
October 7, 2009
EAD encoding:
James Zhang, October 7, 2009
Date modified:
RMC Staff, October 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor in the Dept. of Human Development (1966-1993) and Director of the Center for Research on the Effects of Television.
Condry graduated from West Virigina University with an A.B. degree in 1961 and an M.A. in 1962. He received his PhD in social psychology in 1966 from the University of California at Los Angeles. His entire teaching career was spent at Cornell with sabbaticals at the University of Kansas and at Stanford University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes publications and papers written by Condry concerning his research on the psychology of television and its impact on children.
Cite As:
John Condry papers, #23-13-3741. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Related Materials
See also collections #23-2-2817 and #47-10-3394 for additional biographical information.
Television viewers. Television broadcasting -- Social aspects z United States. Mass media and children. Television and children -- United States. Television broadcasting -- Psychological aspects. Condry, John C. Cornell University -- : Faculty.
